title: Counterbalanced weight lifting apparatus
abstract: A weight lifting apparatus (1) comprising: a frame (3) including a pair of vertical sliding rails (6), a pair of connectors (12) each slidably mounted on a respective sliding rail (6) of the frame (3) and comprising means for securing a barbell (2), and a chain drive (7) including an upper sprocket (8) and a lower sprocket (9) associated with each of the pair of sliding rails (6) rotatably mounted on the frame (3), and a chain (11) trained about the upper sprocket (8) and the lower sprocket (9) associated with the respective sliding rail (6), wherein the ends of the chain (11) are attached to the connector (12) on the respective sliding rail (6), wherein the upper sprockets (8) are interconnected by means of a horizontal rod (10) for synchronising the rotational movement of the chain drive (7), wherein the weight lifting apparatus (1) further comprises a resilient member mounted between the frame (3) and the chain drive (7) or at least one of the connectors (12) and adapted to exert a force on the chain drive(7) or the at least one connector (12) over the range of motion of the connector (12) along the sliding rail (6).
claims: Claims 1. A weight lifting apparatus (1) comprising: a frame (3) including a pair of vertical sliding rails (6), a pair of connectors (12) each slidably mounted on a respective sliding rail (6) of the frame (3) and comprising means for securing a barbell (2), and a chain drive (7) including an upper sprocket (8) and a lower sprocket (9) associated with each of the pair of sliding rails (6) rotatably mounted on the frame (3), and a chain (11) trained about the upper sprocket (8) and the lower sprocket (9) associated with the respective sliding rail (6), wherein the ends of the chain (11) are attached to the connector (12) on the respective sliding rail (6), wherein the upper sprockets (8) are interconnected by means of a horizontal rod (10) for synchronising the rotational movement of the chain drive (7), characterised in that the weight lifting apparatus (1) further comprises a resilient member mounted between the frame (3) and the chain drive (7) or at least one of the connectors (12) and adapted to exert a force on the chain drive (7) or the at least one connector (12) over the range of motion of the connector (12) along the sliding rail (6). 2. The weight lifting apparatus (1) according to claim 1, wherein the resilient member comprises a spring in the form of a rolled ribbon (21) of spring metal arranged in a housing (20) mounted on the top of the frame (3) and connected to one of the connectors (12). 3. The weight lifting apparatus (1) according to claim 1, wherein the resilient member comprises a torsion spring arranged coaxially on the horizontal rod (10), wherein one end of the torsion spring is rigidly mounted in relation to the frame (3) and the other end is mounted to one of the upper sprockets (8) and/or the horizontal rod (10) for co- rotation therewith. 4. The weight lifting apparatus (1) according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising a housing (24) accommodating the horizontal rod (10). 5. The weight lifting apparatus (1) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the stiffness of the resilient member is configured such that the resulting forces acting on the connectors (12) substantially cancel each other out. 6. The weight lifting apparatus (1) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the sliding rails (6) are mounted directly on the frame (3) and present a substantially I or H-shaped cross section comprising a pair of flanges (23) and a web section (22). 7. The weight lifting apparatus (1) according to claim 6, wherein the connectors (12) comprise a fitting (14) adapted to receive the flanges (23) of the sliding rails (6). 8. The weight lifting apparatus (1) according to claim 7, wherein the connectors (12) further comprise one or more roller bearings arranged in the fitting (14) and adapted to roll against the sliding rails (6). 9. The weight lifting apparatus (1) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the connectors (12) comprise means for releasably securing a barbell (2). 10. The weight lifting apparatus (1) according to claim 9, wherein the securing means comprise a recess (18) formed in the connector (12) adapted to receive the barbell (2), and a pivotal member (15) which may be brought between a first position wherein the barbell (2) may be inserted into or removed from the recess (18), and a second position wherein the pivotal member (15) locks the barbell (2) in the recess (18).
